TextENGNew: I grabbed Mion's heavy body by the shoulders and Rena grabbed her by the feet as we loaded her into the cart.
Then we covered her with the blue tarp.
Right before that, I discretely moved the hatchet to the ground. ...All the while praying that Rena didn't notice.
When I raised my head to take a look around, Rena was simply staring at the ground.
...We pretended not to notice one another.
``...Let's get going.@k@vS18/01/160100746. I'll pull the cart this time.@k@vS18/01/160100747. You can push from the back, Rena.``
``Sure. Got it...``
Her response sounded mechanical. Her voice was inorganic, devoid of any emotion.
I casually turned around while Rena was looking the other direction. Then I picked up the hatchet and gently tucked it under the tarp.
``Pushing now.``
Rrr...
The cart nearly crashed into me as it started to move.
We slowly pulled the cart carrying Mion away from the garbage mountain.
I suddenly remembered the day that Rena and I brought the statue back.
I made an offhanded joke at the time about how it felt like we were pushing around a dead body... but I never thought I'd actually be transporting a corpse this way.
And to top it off, it was Mion's body...
This was turning into a farce...
The extreme force I had to project into my hands to pull the cart signified the weight of Mion's existence.
I fought back the urge to cry as I walked to Satoko's house with heavy footsteps.
I spent the whole time praying that we wouldn't run into anyone.
What was Rena thinking back there, while pushing the cart with our stiff cargo?
I'm confident... At least until we reach Satoko's house... there's no risk of her doing something with that kitchen knife. ...Probably.
I cautiously looked back, hoping to discern something of the thoughts Rena kept locked in her heart.
Rena didn't look back at me.
She kept looking downward, concentrating solely on the task of walking forward, one step at a time.
Right...
Seeing Rena's emotionless expression was unbearably disheartening.
When did this happen...? My stomach churned because there were too many people whose thoughts I couldn't comprehend. What was Rena thinking right now?
I couldn't trust anyone. ......It was a hopeless thing to say, but I couldn't allow anyone to make things worse.
changeReason: Old uses present tense 'I can't trust anyone'; new uses past tense 'I couldn't trust anyone.' JPN 信じられない is present tense, matching the old version's immediacy; the new shifts to retrospective past tense.

TextENGNew: I wanted so badly to talk to Mion, who was lying dead in the cargo rack. If she was dead, there was some peace to be gained from that. ...She'll never have a sudden change of heart and try to kill me.
Hey Mion, please listen to me. Honestly, I'm afraid of Rena...
At this point, I felt it was safe to tell Mion anything.
It was such an ironic relationship, where we couldn't trust each other until one of us was dead. Does that really qualify as a trusting relationship...?
``...Kei-chan, are you an idiot~!?``
``Of course that's not a trusting relationship!``
``It's not friendship or anything!``
I felt like I could hear Mion's voice.
...That's true. If I couldn't bring myself to trust someone while they were still alive, that didn't sound like friendship at all.
``...But don't worry!``
``Because this old man is rooting for you, Kei-chan!``
``Because I'm your friend!``
``No matter what!``
Mion's cheerful voice called out to me, over and over.
``...Good luck, Kei-chan!``
``Do your best, Kei-chan!``
``Keep going, Kei-chan!``
I stared back at Rena as she walked forward with a blank expression...
Even though Mion was lying dead under the tarp, it felt like she was encouraging me.
How selfish of me. ...Only finding my courage after a dead person encouraged me.
Even though Rena and I were actually the ones who killed Mion...
My ears were ringing.
It couldn't be helped... I had no choice.
I started feeling sick.
I didn't want to find myself in a situation where I needed that concealed hatchet... And I didn't want Rena to have an opportunity to use her concealed knife...
Honestly, I didn't want to kill anyone else...
I wanted to have lots of friends. To laugh and cry together with all my living friends...
...What are you thinking?! Don't lose heart, you swore an oath!
I sighed and shook my head.
The time for those sweet dreams has already passed! Open your eyes, Keiichi Maebara!
That era of playing around and having a pleasant time with your perfect group of friends is already a thing of the past.
......Kill before being killed. Otherwise, you'll get killed. That's all there is to it.
As I stared at Rena's slender figure, my heart slowly filled with wretched murderous intent.
Once again, the voice of the crickets filled my ears.
Shut up... Leave me alone...
The more I wished for their silence, the louder the crickets cried out, like they were trying to criticize me...
We managed to travel from the garbage mountain to the Houjou house without running into anyone.
The surrounding area was completely swallowed by the dusk. Everything around me looked gray.
I was a bit surprised to notice that the kitchen window was still broken.
That's no good... It will be problematic if the neighbors start looking around and stumble upon the corpses.
I'll have to fix that window as soon as possible...
``Don't worry, Keiichi-kun.``
Rena reached into her pocket and pulled out the key.
``...Oh, this?@k@vS18/02/160200576. I found it while we were here yesterday, so I brought it home with me.``
Rena slid the key into the keyhole.
*clank*
Then the door opened like normal.
``Don't worry about the window.@k@vS18/02/160200578. Rena has a plan.``
Rena's expression as we entered the house indicated that she could see right through my thoughts.
I hesitated for a moment to decide if I should take my shoes off... but I chose to leave them on and followed her inside.
The inside smelled as bad as ever.
Rena looked eerily dignified while she calmly looked back at me and surveyed the area.
``Let's set Mii-chan down right next to them so all three are lined up.``
I was confused when I heard that, but I quickly responded.
``Aah, right...``
I did my best to enunciate every word in my typical intonation to avoid agitating her.
``What's wrong, Keiichi-kun...?``
``...Oh... nothing.@k@vS18/01/160100750. I'll go get Mion.``
I said that in a light tone like I was talking about carrying in the luggage, then I went back to the cart to get Mion.
Mion felt like a heavy slab of meat as I carried her on my back. ...Urrgh.
I fiddled with the hatchet to keep it securely inside the tarp as I struggled to walk forward.
Unfortunately... the hatchet was far too big to keep it hidden.
...It can't be helped. If it becomes necessary, I'll have no choice but to keep going. It's not such a big deal.
Just to be safe, I adjusted the tarp on the side facing Rena.
*slam*
Something slipped, and a kitchen knife fell to the ground.
I gently picked up the knife.
...Huh? ...Does that mean Rena isn't carrying the knife...?
The knife that killed Mion was still soaked in blood.
A single long strand of Mion's hair was stuck to that blood.
It bothered me, so I tried to peel Mion's hair off, but the hair was so firmly caked into the blood that I couldn't get it off.
Well, whatever...
I concealed the kitchen knife in my left hand and made my way inside the house once again.
When I came back inside, Rena was in the kitchen lighting the stove.
``...Welcome back.@k@vS18/02/160200582. ...Took you a while, didn't it?``
``Yeah, it's heavy and kind of a pain.``
I answered nonchalantly, then set Mion down between Satoko and Shion.
I couldn't bear to see these three girls reduced to mere objects, so I found a sheet in the closet and laid it on top of them.
For a moment, it looked like a group of girls having a sleepover.
``Good night...@k@vS18/01/160100753. You can rest easy now...``
I held my hands together in a prayer.
Mion... It looks like you're all having pleasant dreams together...
I hope everyone is having fun and doing club activities together, living our peaceful everyday life in some other world... That is my only wish...
Sentimental feelings rushed through me, and tears began streaming out.
...Though they looked like they were only sleeping, in truth the three of them were dead. They could move no longer...
She took a pile of letters out of the mailbox and carried them into the kitchen earlier.
She held a stack of them together over the stove.
``Oh, this?@k@vS18/02/160200584. I'm burning the house down.@k@vS18/02/160200585. ...After all, we can't leave everyone this way, can we?``
The calm tone in Rena's voice sent a chill through me.
I was struck by the feeling that I was all on my own, which might actually be advantageous given the current situation.
...But even so. These are our friends, right?
``This...@k@vS18/02/160200587. All of this is your fault, Keiichi-kun......``
A deep voice I could scarcely believe belonged to Rena came from the kitchen.
The voice was so eerie that I couldn't bring myself to turn and look back at the source.
``Just like Keiichi-kun, Rena wants to shed tears to mourn everyone's deaths.@k@vS18/02/160200589. ...Just like Keiichi-kun, Rena didn't really want to do this either.``
``I thought that killing Teppei would allow Satoko to come to school again. And then we'd be able to regain our peaceful everyday life.``
I felt the same way...
But I decided to hold my tongue.
Rena quickly lowered a letter toward the stove. She did so in a careful, mechanical motion. Very precisely.
The letter smoldered and crackled on the stove.
Then Rena picked up another letter with one hand and held it over the stove.

``I told you to keep it a secret.... but you talked to Shii-chan, didn't you?@k@vS18/02/160200592. I think that was the beginning of the end.@k@vS18/02/160200593. ...There were several mistakes along the way, but don't you think that's where it all started...?``
Rena glared at me with the flames of the stove flickering in her eyes.
I thought that Rena was just muttering to vent hard feelings. ...But the next moment...
``Do you understand, Keiichi-kun...?@k@vS18/02/160200595. Because once you told Shii-chan... she became involved.``

``Shii-chan died because she was taking care of Satoko-chan.@k@vS18/02/160200597. ...And Shii-chan's death led to Mii-chan's death.@k@vS18/02/160200598. In the end, we couldn't help Satoko-chan. She died too.@k@vS18/02/160200599. Because you got Rika-chan involved...``
``......In other words, every unfortunate event had a clear cause.``
``I'm sorry...``
Once I heard Rika-chan's name, I was struck with a sense that something seemed wrong, but my guilt urged me to apologize first.
``You don't need to apologize.@k@vS18/02/160200602. Keiichi-kun... there was nothing you could have done. Right?@k@vS18/02/160200603. ...So don't worry about it.``

``You're not in the wrong, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200605. ......This was fate.@k@vS18/02/160200606. This was our destiny, predetermined from the moment we were born.@k@vS18/02/160200607. Ahahahahahahahahaha.``
Rena picked up a half-burned letter from the stove and dropped it.
A nasty smell rose up, irritating my nose.
Rena, stop playing with the fire... If you're going to burn it, do it quickly. Otherwise the smell will drift to the surrounding area.
I thought that to myself as I carefully watched Rena's hands. But for some reason, I couldn't bring myself to say anything.
When Rena grew tired of playing with the letters, she grabbed a jug of vegetable oil from the cupboard under the stove.
She poured a thin line in a circle around the stovetop.
``This... is Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
Rena had a cold-hearted smile on her face, the likes of which I'd never seen before.
What's going on...? Why is Rena smiling at a time like this?
Unlike the three dead girls sleeping like angels in the back room, the Rena standing in front of me looked like a cruel demon.
``This is Rena's punishment.@k@vS18/02/160200610. For angering Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS18/02/160200611. ...Everyone died because Rena depended on Keiichi-kun.``
``And when Rena took advantage of Keiichi-kun, Oyashiro-sama used Keiichi-kun's body to punish Rena.``
``...Hey, Rena. Are you all right?@k@vS18/01/160100759. What's gotten into you?``
``Ahahahahahahahahahahahaha!@k@vS18/02/160200614. Why are you suddenly getting so worked up? Worked up?``
``Do you think Rena has gone mad?@k@vS18/02/160200616. I won't make any mistakes this time.``

``...No matter what happens, Rena won't give in.@k@vS18/02/160200618. When you used the word ``destiny`` a while back, Rena realized it.@k@vS18/02/160200619. ...That you should have never been trusted to begin with.``

``That's when I realized my first mistake was relying on you.@k@vS18/02/160200621. But I've already come to understand that, so it'll be ok.@k@vS18/02/160200622. This time, this time I'll be able to do it!``
``Hey, Rena...?@k@vS18/01/160100761. What do you mean? What are you going to do...?``
Anxiety steadily piled up inside my chest.
Rena lit the stove.
The oil around the stove ignited and the flame soared up.
All the oil around the stove lit up in a flash and the entire kitchen was covered in flames in an instant.

``Rena... will not give in.@k@vS18/02/160200624. Rena will never forgive Oyashiro-sama, or anyone else who tries to destroy our happiness.@k@vS18/02/160200625. So Rena will keep fighting.@k@vS18/02/160200626. If I've lost it, I'll build it from scratch all over again.``
``Even if it's destroyed again and again, Rena will keep fighting to grab hold of that happiness...!``
Seeing the passion in Rena's eyes made me want to run away.
Rena has gone crazy...!
Rena was so exhausted with everything that's happened that it drove her to the brink of insanity.
I need to get out of this house. Rena has finally gone mad.

``Rena...! Let's go home already.@k@vS18/01/160100763. Let's get out of here for now.@k@vS18/01/160100764. You look like you need some rest.@k@vS18/01/160100765. That's all.@k@vS18/01/160100766. That's all there is to it.``

``What are you talking about?@k@vS18/02/160200629. Rena isn't tired at all.@k@vS18/02/160200630. ...Besides, even if you're feeling tired you still have to work hard.@k@vS18/02/160200631. ...Rena will not depend on others any longer.@k@vS18/02/160200632. I'll never give up, never cry, and never run away.``

``We had such fun everyday lives at one point.@k@vS18/02/160200634. So I'll definitely... get those back...!@k@vS18/02/160200635. Whatever it takes to secure Rena's happiness!@k@vS18/02/160200636. ...I won't let Oyashiro-sama get in my way!!``
Rena slowly stepped forward from the flames with a slight smile on her face.
As though the heat didn't faze her at all.
``Rena, I understand...@k@vS18/01/160100768. I get it...``
``Ahahahahahahaha.@k@vS18/02/160200638. Keiichi-kun, there's no need to worry.@k@vS18/02/160200639. ...Rena will be all right.@k@vS18/02/160200640. Rena absolutely will not die.``
``I can't die until I retake my happiness, with my own two hands.@k@vS18/02/160200642. I won't let Oyashiro-sama get in the way!@k@vS18/02/160200643. This time, it's Rena's turn to help everyone.``
This is bad...!
Everything became clear to me. Even if I tried talking to Rena, there was no way it would get through to her.
It's like the person before me was another being, wearing Rena's face as a mask. I wasn't sure what to do...... but I understood that she was dangerous!
Run away...! I have to run away...!
I rushed off toward the entrance.
But the moment I took a step, Rena started to move.
``Keiichi-kun...!``
Rena lunged straight toward my chest.
Taken off-guard by her surprisingly gentle touch, I quickly grabbed Rena's slender body with both my hands.
``Keiichi-kun...@k@vS18/02/160200646. I've finally... got you...``
``...Let's go, together.``
I spoke gently while holding her tight with both my hands.
Please, let my thoughts get through to her...
We could recruit new club members at school, and regain our happy days that way. Right? And you could lead the club this time, Rena.
Right, Rena...?
I tightly embraced Rena's frail body with both my arms.
This was the first time in my life I'd ever hugged a girl so tight.
``Aahahahahahahahahahahahaha!``
At first, my body seemed to go numb.
Then, slowly... a warmth spread through my stomach like I was being gouged with a burning torch.

``What are you talking about?! Talking about?!@k@vS18/02/160200649. Don't you realize Rena has already stopped trusting Keiichi-kun?@k@vS18/02/160200650. I guess you misunderstood? ...Misunderstood?@k@vS18/02/160200651. Ahahahahahahahahaha!``
My body was burning up, but it wasn't just from the heat of the flame-soaked room.
The heat was pouring out of my stomach. When I touched it with my hand, I felt an odd sensation along my palm.
...There was something... sticking into my stomach......
``It's the kitchen knife you brought, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200653. The same one that stabbed Mii-chan earlier.``
``Th-that's...``
You're wrong, Rena. You're the one who brought that knife!
``You were planning to kill Rena, weren't you?@k@vS18/02/160200655. But Rena knew all about that.@k@vS18/02/160200656. You put that kitchen knife on the cart, didn't you?``
``You're wro...``
When I tried speaking, it felt like even more blood came flowing out of my stomach.

``You still don't understand, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200658. Didn't Rena already say that she didn't trust you?@k@vS18/02/160200659. I don't believe in fate.@k@vS18/02/160200660. And I'm not afraid of Oyashiro-sama.``
``...Rena will grab hold of her happiness again, using her own two hands!@k@vS18/02/160200662. That's my only wish!! Aahahahahahahahahahah!!!``
Rena's terrifying smile made me lose all the strength in my body.
What was I to Rena...? What did I do...?

I pulled out the knife lodged in my stomach and tossed it away. ...Aah, it was the knife from this house. The same knife that killed Shion and Satoko, then took Mion's life. And in the end it stabbed me too.
My consciousness was quickly fading.

``Keiichi-kun, you were just carrying out Oyashiro-sama's orders, weren't you?@k@vS18/02/160200664. You were only trying to kill Rena because you were manipulated by Oyashiro-sama, right?@k@vS18/02/160200665. It's all right, Rena understands.``
``I know that in reality, you're not the kind of person who'd do something so awful.@k@vS18/02/160200667. I understand that.@k@vS18/02/160200668. I understand, so it'll be all right.``
``...I've saved you from Oyashiro-sama...@k@vS18/02/160200670. With that kitchen knife!!``
I had no idea what Rena was talking about.
I was being manipulated by Oyashiro-sama? Being saved through death? Rena, what are you talking about? You're the one who's acting strange.

``But still, Rena was a little hurt, you know...?@k@vS18/02/160200672. Because Keiichi-kun was planning to kill Rena with that knife...@k@vS18/02/160200673. Up until the end, Rena thought that Keiichi-kun was a precious friend...``
``I was thinking of helping poor, poor Keiichi-kun, but...``
That can't be...? Rena still thought of me as a precious friend?
``Farewell, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200676. ...You can apologize if you'd like.``
Rena... If I apologize, will you forgive me?
You're wrong... You're wrong, Rena. It's not my fault. I wasn't plotting anything evil. I swear. You're wrong... It's just a terrible misunderstanding, Rena...
Every time I spoke, I could feel an unsettling sensation flowing out of my stomach. But I couldn't stop apologizing.
If Rena wants me to apologize, I'll do it as much as I can. ...If that's what it takes to take back our everyday happiness.
``Ahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahaha.``
``...It's no good. Even if you apologize, it's pointless.``
``It's pointless!!``
``Aahahahahahahahahaha!!!``
I heard the sound of the front door opening and closing.
Then the room got quiet. At that point, I could only hear the vague sounds of the crackling fire and the burning room.
I'm going to die... right here.
Things get quieter on the verge of death...
Cold... So cold... Hey Mion, I guess this is what death feels like...
Satoko... Shion... Rika-chan...... Rena...
